summary: Kristen, you crushed a 2-hour endurance ride averaging 245W normalized — that's 82% of your FTP with solid pacing. Your efficiency factor of 1.73 and low cardiac drift of 3.2% show strong aerobic fitness.

insights.title: Normalized Power 245W at 82% IF — textbook endurance pace
insights.body: Your NP of 245W with an IF of 0.822 is right in the sweet spot for aerobic development rides. The variability index of 1.14 shows smooth, steady output — great pacing discipline.

insights.title: Low cardiac drift of 3.2% — aerobic engine firing well
insights.body: Your HR drift of just 3.2% over 2 hours indicates excellent aerobic fitness and likely adequate fueling. For comparison, your hill repeats on Feb 26 showed 7.4% drift at a higher IF (0.96), which is expected for that intensity.

insights.title: HRV 62ms is above your 7-day average of 57ms
insights.body: Your HRV trending above baseline suggests good recovery status coming into this ride. Your sleep score of 82 with 7.8 hours and 22% deep sleep provided a solid foundation. This may explain the strong efficiency factor.

insights.title: TSB at -25.8 — accumulating fatigue, monitor recovery
insights.body: Your training stress balance has been negative for the past week (range: -10 to -35). While this is normal during a build phase, consider a lighter day if your HRV drops below 50ms or you notice RPE creeping up at the same power.

insights.title: Estimated 1680 kcal burned — aim for 60-90g carbs/hr on rides 2hr+
insights.body: At 245W NP for 2 hours, you likely burned ~60% carbohydrate. Ensuring 60-90g carbs/hr during the ride helps maintain power output and reduces recovery time. Your current ferritin of 65 ng/mL is in the athlete-optimal range, supporting oxygen delivery.

dataGaps: Connect Oura Ring for detailed sleep stage tracking to correlate deep sleep with next-day power output
Add body composition tracking via Withings for accurate W/kg and race weight planning
Upload a DEXA scan for lean mass W/kg — more accurate than scale-based estimates
